text: After the viewing port is placed, a second trocar is inserted so that a working channel can be created.
We count down from the first rib in order to identify the rib plan for thoracoplasty.
A hot knife is used to remove the overlying parietal pleura and soft tissue from the surface of the rib.
After freeing the rib, it is cut distally.
Subsequently, the rib head is then dissected free and disarticulated proximally from the transverse process.
The rib is then removed and pulled out through the working channel.
This rib removal process is repeated as needed
